description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with a single "build" stage containing numerous build jobs (basic_syntax_check_clang, build_macos_arm, build_macos_intel, build_ubuntu_1804, build_ubuntu_2004, build_ubuntu_2204, build_ubuntu_2404, build_ubuntu_curr_clang15, build_ubuntu_curr_clang16, build_ubuntu_curr_clang17, build_ubuntu_curr_clang18, build_ubuntu_curr_clang19, build_ubuntu_curr_gcc9, build_ubuntu_curr_gcc10, build_ubuntu_curr_gcc11, build_ubuntu_curr_gcc12, build_ubuntu_curr_gcc13, build_ubuntu_curr_gcc13_arm32, build_ubuntu_curr_gcc13_arm64, build_ubuntu_curr_gcc14, build_windows) each using the appropriate Ubuntu, macOS or Windows Docker image, exporting CCACHE variables in before_script, pulling/pushing a .ccache cache keyed by job name, and running the shared script sequence: remove and recreate a build directory, cmake with static, OpenCV, serialdev and examples flags, then make (with -j1, -j4 or -j8) and optionally make install on master branches. The pipeline also includes a "pages" job in the build stage that runs on an Ubuntu docs image, builds documentation with python3 docs-build.py, caches docs/build, and publishes the public/ artifact on tags or branches. A "pre-commit_check" job runs pre-commit on the rolling Ubuntu image for any branch. Deployment jobs in a "deploy" stage include "release_archive_submodules" (archives source, uploads to the GitLab package registry and AWS S3), "release_arch" (runs arch-release.sh on an Arch Linux image using SSH keys and VERSION_NAME), and "release_fedora" (runs on a Fedora image with COPR API key) which all trigger on tags or manual web pipelines with RELEASE variables. All jobs use rules to run on merge requests or commit branches, have interruptible settings, appropriate runner tags (macos, windows, docker, qemu), and set environment variables such as CC, CXX, PATH, MSYSTEM, GIT_STRATEGY, and VERSION_NAME derived from RELEASE_VERSION or CI_COMMIT_TAG.
